prompt_inputs.goal: Muscle recovery and strength gains from weightlifting training

solution_criteria: Meal plan covers all meals for one full day
Protein is distributed across at least 4 meals/snacks with minimum 30g per serving
Total daily protein meets or exceeds 1.6g per kg of body weight (136g)
Includes post-workout recovery nutrition with carbohydrates and protein
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
scenario: Testing with a strength-based athlete (weightlifter) with specific protein requirements and muscle recovery needs, verifying the plan includes adequate protein distribution across meals and recovery nutrition

prompt_inputs.goal: Early morning swim training (5:00 AM start) with energy for 90-minute high-intensity session

solution_criteria: Includes a pre-dawn meal (before 5:00 AM) that is easily digestible and won't cause stomach discomfort during swimming
Provides adequate hydration strategy throughout the day with specific fluid recommendations
All meals and snacks comply with stated dietary restrictions
Plan is compact and practical for a single day with realistic preparation time constraints
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
scenario: Testing with a sport-specific constraint scenario (swimmer with early morning training) requiring pre-dawn meal options and hydration focus, ensuring practicality for unusual training schedules

prompt_inputs.goal: Marathon training with carbohydrate loading for high-endurance performance

solution_criteria: Meal plan contains 4-5 meals/snacks distributed throughout the day with timing around training
Prioritizes energy-dense, carbohydrate-rich foods (pasta, rice, oats, bread) comprising at least 60% of calories
Includes protein sources for muscle recovery and hydration guidance
Estimated daily caloric intake aligns with marathon runner requirements (3000-3500 calories)
task_description: Write a compact, concise 1 day meal plan for a single athlete.
scenario: Testing with a high-endurance athlete (marathon runner) requiring high calorie intake and carbohydrate loading, ensuring the meal plan prioritizes energy-dense foods and timing around training
